raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Remote public participation for governments is vital for individuals who would otherwise be excluded from decision-making spaces.",
    "Existing legislation requires local elected officials who choose to teleconference to make their private addresses known if there is no majority vote to waive this requirement.",
    "The ability to teleconference into meetings without revealing private addresses could lead to reduced travel and childcare costs for members.",
    "Enabling remote participation through teleconferencing could increase member participation in local government meetings.",
    "The implementation of livestreams and call-in or video options for public participation can benefit traditionally underrepresented groups, such as working families and people with disabilities.",
    "Allowing members of a Brown Act body to teleconference without revealing private addresses may diversify the composition of board members and commissioners."
  ]
}
```
